Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to generate 20 open ended thought provoking questions about the given pseudo BPMN process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The questions should delve into the rationale behind process flows potential improvements risk management decision making stakeholder communication and performance measurement

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The prompt says Given the pseudo BPMN representation generate 20 open ended thought provoking questions that delve deeper into the rationale behind the process flows potential improvements risk management decision making stakeholder communication and performance measurement Do not provide any SQL queries just list the questions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process start with market research RandD design prototype parallel gateway split to secure components from various countries Then after all secured join ship to assembly plant in Malaysia assemble exclusive gateway quality checks then pack and label parallel split air freight and cargo ship join customs clearance receive at regional distribution centers parallel split distribution tasks join monitor inventory and sales forecast exclusive gateway adjust orders or proceed to next sales cycle end

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Think about each part Ill craft questions that explore why the process is designed this way what could go wrong how to optimize communication between stakeholders KPIs etc

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible angles Why parallel securing of components from different geographic locations What if one supplier delayed Could there be a risk pooling or buffer strategy How to coordinate Decision making for supplier selection

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Assembly 24 7 operation what risks does continuous operation pose equipment failure labor How to manage quality in a high speed environment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Exclusive gateway for quality how is the pass fail threshold determined What feedback loop to RandD How to balance speed and quality Rework vs scrap decision criteria

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Distribution split between air freight and cargo ship what is the cost speed trade off How are markets classified as high demand vs other Could there be a dynamic adjustment based on real time demand How to handle last minute changes

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Customs clearance how to ensure compliance across many countries Risks of delays duties How to streamline

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Regional distribution centers why these four regions How to handle cross border shipments within regions Coordination with local partners

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Parallel distribution tasks warehousing retailers ecommerce VIP how to manage inventory allocation Conflicts between channels How to prioritize VIP orders without alienating retailers

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Monitor inventory and sales forecast how is forecast generated Who does it What data sources How does it trigger adjusting orders to suppliers What is the decision rule for demand changes vs stable How to avoid bullwhip effect

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Communication between RandD procurement assembly logistics sales How to align incentives

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Performance measurement what KPIs along the supply chain How to measure end to end cycle time on time delivery cost per unit yield inventory turns customer satisfaction How to attribute delays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Risk management geopolitical risks natural disasters trade wars pandemics How to build resilience

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Supplier diversification are there single source components How to qualify alternative suppliers What if a key component becomes unavailable

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Sustainability carbon footprint of air vs sea shipping packaging ecommerce returns How could the process be greener

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Technology how could IoT blockchain AI for demand forecasting and quality control improve the process

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Decision making authority who decides to rework vs scrap who decides to shift from sea to air how to empower local distribution centers

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The process shows a linear flow but with parallel gateways In reality could some tasks be overlapped Could assembly start before all components secured What is the rationale for strict join before shipping to assembly

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The exclusive gateway after monitoring inventory says adjust orders to suppliers if demand changes How is this feedback loop integrated with ongoing production How to balance lead times

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The process includes direct shipments for VIP pre orders what qualifies as VIP How to manage exclusivity and avoid channel conflict

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The join after parallel distribution tasks suggests monitor inventory and sales forecast occurs after all distributions done In reality monitoring would be ongoing so the model may be simplified

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Question about timing and continuous monitoring

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The RandD prototype how does market research feed into design Is there a feedback loop after quality checks or sales to improve next generation Is this a closed loop product lifecycle

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Why assembly in Malaysia labor cost trade agreements location Risk of concentration

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Customs clearance how to handle multiple product variants with different regulations
